quote_of_day.text: Having a sacred animal is the most retarded thing a culture could do. Eating beef is based, it's just a fucking animal. If you think it's a god get your malnutritioned brain checked. >muh milk Every other mammal gives milk, why is cow special.
quote_of_day.name: Ambedkar
quote_of_day.topic: critique of sacred animals, dietary choices

We need more anons like iac to make cool shit. Im a very impressed pajeet. Writing this to see if it will get featured on the newspaper or not, im not sure how he set it up but if the !thing works then hello indus news! Im on da news niggaaaaaa
guest_letters.name: Narthie

articles.title: Weather for Indus today
articles.body:
A low-pressure system of blasphemy and dietary debate moved into the board this morning, centered on sacred animals and faith.

The discussion was dense and contentious, with one anon calling the concept of sacred animals 'retarded' and beef-eating 'based.' A weak front of Chan culture promotion pushed into the region, with an anon advocating for the spread of Chan culture in India to attract 'real-deal femcels.' The push was light and received little traction.

A scattered high-pressure system of social media promotion touched down briefly, with Ambedkar's call to shill the website on Instagram and Reddit.

Meanwhile, Fuwawa issued a warning against responding to spammers, and an anon shared AI-generated saree images in a brief, isolated downpour of creativity.

articles.title: Anon debates sacred animals, dietary laws, and faith
articles.body:
Ambedkar opened the thread by criticizing the concept of sacred animals, calling it "the most retarded thing a culture could do." They argued that eating beef is acceptable, and that cows are just animals, not gods.

Anon responded by suggesting that the issue is not just about personal beliefs, but also about the spiritual and moral frameworks that people operate within.

They noted that even if one doesn't care about animal welfare, the spiritual layer of their faith is a separate consideration.

Another anon countered that cows are not considered gods, but rather protected animals, and that the concept of "Aghniya" is often misunderstood. They argued that the protection of cows is a matter of national law and cultural tradition.

The discussion also touched on genetics and the Y chromosome, with one anon disputing the claim that the Y chromosome is weaker than the X.

They explained that the Y and X chromosomes are fundamentally the same, but evolved differently in mammals. Narthie later expressed frustration with the thread, stating that it was going off track and being ruined by a few "retards".

Anon also discussed their plans to recreate the Indus Valley Diet, which includes meat and seafood, millets, and buffalo milk. They argued that this diet would have physiological benefits and could be a way to connect with their cultural heritage.

The thread also saw several exchanges on the nature of faith, morality, and dietary laws. One anon noted that choosing to follow certain dietary laws is a matter of faith, while another argued that it's about morality.

Soythie brought up the point that different religions have different taboos, and that Muslims, for example, are easily offended by the existence of kafir societies.

Dyaush later added that the sanctity of cows is not just about their utility, but about their spiritual significance, which exists independently of their practical uses.

Throughout the thread, various anons debated and discussed the intersection of faith, culture, and personal choice, with no clear resolution in sight.

articles.title: Anon Pushes for Chan Culture in India
articles.body:
Soyjeet posted about promoting Chan culture in India to attract what he called 'genuine foids' or female incels, distinguishing them from what he described as 'ugly reddit gobartroons.' Anon Bhondu responded, discussing genetics and upbringing, stating that men's gene code is superior and that women are 'like water.' Bhondu claimed that men are 'hard wired to act a certain way, while women are like water.' Chuddie commented that women 'want to be raped' and that they 'risk letting us sniff their digital foot prints' by being online.

Chuddie also made a graphic comment about what he would do if he could 'stop time,' targeting feminist page admins. Momojeet interjected, suggesting that promoting Chan culture in India might not be feasible, citing his own experiences with girls who have used imageboards.

Momojeet mentioned that the girls he knows are either lesbian or bisexual.

Chuddie continued to express his views, describing women as 'inferior men' and stating that the Y chromosome makes men 'taller, muscular, smarter.' Chuddie also made several comments about rape and women, including a remark about making feminist page admins pregnant.

Haggu chimed in, stating that 'jeetas can't comprehend incel humour,' and that if you talk about rape, they will take it literally and create many 'randi-rona posts' on Reddit.

The conversation also touched on the topic of women using imageboards, with Momojeet noting that girls who use these platforms are often not straight.

Chuddie claimed that if a woman makes sense on an imageboard, she is likely a man, and that women have not contributed to these platforms unless they can rely on their bodies or attention-seeking.

articles.title: Ambedkar pushes site promotion on social media
articles.body:
Ambedkar kicked off the discussion by suggesting that the site needs more traffic and should be promoted on Instagram and Reddit. He personally offered to help with this effort.

In response to another post, Ambedkar clarified that he would try to improve the site's UI to make it more appealing and to feature quality posters.

He also mentioned that he would address low-level design issues and thanked users for their feedback. The conversation took a different turn when Soyjeet and Yui weighed in, criticizing certain groups on Instagram and Reddit.

Soyjeet called some of these groups "retarded" and "clout-hungry whore spawns," while Yui noted that some Instagram users are "based" but that Reddit is infested with Dalits.

Other users, such as Soythie and Nepajeet, offered suggestions on how to promote the site, including paying for Google ads or Reddit ads.

However, Gura responded with a dismissive comment, saying that old users are tired of debating the same topics and that newcomers will find the site anyway.

Ambedkar continued to engage with users, discussing topics such as spoilers on the homepage and the need for a new CAPTCHA.

He also mentioned that he had already tried shilling the site on 4chan's /bharat/ board and appreciated his friends for their help. The discussion concluded with various users sharing their thoughts on site promotion, moderation, and design.

Ambedkar reiterated his commitment to improving the site and making it more appealing to users.

articles.title: Fuwawa warns against responding to spammer
articles.body: Fuwawa urged users not to reply to a spammer from /r9k/, calling them a "madarchod" who doesn't understand the board. Fuwawa suggested ignoring the spammer to prevent manual deletion of the thread. Nepajeet translated Fuwawa's original post, explaining that zero replies would automatically delete the thread. Other users, like Motivanon, found the spammer's use of mixed language "genius." The conversation also involved discussions about the board's organization and user anonymity.

articles.title: Anon Creates and Shares AI-Generated Saree Images
articles.body: Aizen sparked discussion with an image post. Nepajeet responded, criticizing Aizen's content creation, saying they waste their imaginative abilities. Kantabai Ownes suggested Aizen could create better content, like AI-generated videos, and offered to provide material. Others, such as Soythie and Motivanon, reacted negatively to Aizen's post. Soythie called it unhot and suggested posting actual porn, while Motivanon joked about AI not knowing how to wear a saree.
